mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a critical threshold, it signals increasing cost pressures and potential for aggressive monetary tightening, which typically dampens corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change exceeds 4.0% but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 7-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and low unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and UNRATE conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of sustained monetary tightening, indicated by a rising federal funds rate, borrowing costs increase for corporations and consumers, compressing profit margins and reducing investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.5 percentage points over 6 months
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 6 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during such cycles, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown, which can negatively impact consumer wealth and confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 5% over 3 months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth combined with low unemployment indicates a strong and efficient economy, fostering high corporate profitability and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 growth and UNRATE conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 7-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential recession, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.
